endpoints.description: Local health endpoint used only for internal diagnostics/tests. Must return JSON: {"status":"ok"}.
Offline-only no-op endpoint placeholder for contract completeness. Must return JSON error with code=AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED.
Offline-only no-op endpoint placeholder for contract completeness. Must return JSON error with code=AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED.

data_models.name: ErrorResponse
data_models.description: Canonical error shape used by any endpoint-like surface (including no-op auth endpoints).

fields.name: error.code
fields.description: Stable machine-readable error code (e.g., AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED, INVALID_ARGUMENT).

fields.name: error.message
fields.description: Human-readable message safe to show to users.

data_models.name: VerseRef
data_models.description: A stable reference to a specific verse.

data_models.name: VerseRange
data_models.description: Inclusive range of verses in a single book. Chapter may differ between start and end (range allowed across chapters).

fields.name: end
fields.description: Range end (inclusive). Must not be before start.

data_models.name: Bookmark
data_models.description: User bookmark for a verse range with optional note.

data_models.name: Highlight
data_models.description: User highlight for a verse range with a color.

fields.name: color
fields.description: One of: YELLOW, GREEN, BLUE, PINK, ORANGE, PURPLE (case-sensitive).

fields.name: font_size_sp
fields.description: Font size in sp. Must be within app-defined bounds.

fields.name: red_letter_enabled
fields.description: If true, render red-letter segments in red where supported by assets.

data_models.name: LastLocation
data_models.description: Last reading location persisted locally for Continue Reading.

fields.name: ref
fields.description: Last verse location (may represent chapter start if verse unknown).

fields.name: updated_at_epoch_ms
fields.description: Timestamp when last location was stored.

data_models.name: BibleAsset
data_models.description: Expected minimal asset structure for Bible text loaded from android/app/src/main/assets/bible/kjv.json. Used for parsing/indexing; not stored in Room.

data_models.name: VerseAsset
data_models.description: Bible verse in asset file, optionally with red-letter segments.

fields.name: text
fields.description: Full verse text as plain string (always present).

fields.name: red_letter_ranges
fields.description: Optional list of UTF-16 code-unit [start,end) ranges within 'text' to render in red.

data_models.name: Range
data_models.description: Half-open character range for highlighting within a string.

data_models.name: VerseOfTheDayScheduleAsset
data_models.description: Expected minimal asset structure for VOTD schedule loaded from android/app/src/main/assets/votd/votd.json.

fields.name: timezone
fields.description: IANA timezone id used for day calculation (e.g., 'UTC' or 'America/New_York').

fields.name: days
fields.description: List length 366 max. Mapping is deterministic and repeats yearly if desired.

data_models.name: VotdDay
data_models.description: A single day mapping entry in the schedule.

database.notes: Room-backed on-device SQLite only. No network sync. Bible text and VOTD schedule are assets and are NOT stored in SQLite.
database.ddl:
PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;

-- User bookmarks (verse ranges + optional note)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  start_book_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  start_chapter INTEGER NOT NULL,
  start_verse INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_book_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  end_chapter INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_verse INTEGER NOT NULL,
  note TEXT,
  created_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  updated_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bookmarks_start ON bookmarks(start_book_id, start_chapter, start_verse);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bookmarks_end ON bookmarks(end_book_id, end_chapter, end_verse);

-- User highlights (verse ranges + color)
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS highlights (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  start_book_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  start_chapter INTEGER NOT NULL,
  start_verse INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_book_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  end_chapter INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_verse INTEGER NOT NULL,
  color TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  updated_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_highlights_start ON highlights(start_book_id, start_chapter, start_verse);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_highlights_end ON highlights(end_book_id, end_chapter, end_verse);

-- Simple preferences storage (settings + last location) in one table.
-- Keys are stable constants in code.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS preferences (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  value TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL
);

-- Required preference keys (value JSON):
-- 'reading_settings' => ReadingSettings JSON
-- 'last_location'    => LastLocation JSON

invariants: All clients use the same endpoint paths and JSON fields as defined here.
No renaming of routes, fields, or tables without updating contract.
Backend must return JSON errors in a consistent shape.
Session strategy is NONE: no login, no auth tokens, no cookies, and no network sync. Any auth endpoints are no-op placeholders returning ErrorResponse with code=AUTH_NOT_SUPPORTED.
App is fully offline-first: all scripture text and verse-of-the-day data must be loaded from assets; app must not require network access to function.
Room (SQLite) is the only persistence mechanism for user data (bookmarks/highlights/preferences/last location). Do not store Bible text in Room.

Asset format is contractual: bible/kjv.json must parse into BibleAsset; votd/votd.json must parse into VerseOfTheDayScheduleAsset. Any format change requires updating CONTRACT.md and parsing code together.
Search must support partial-word matching (substring match) and return match ranges suitable for UI highlighting; matching must be case-insensitive using Unicode-aware lowercasing.
Red-letter rendering must be driven solely by VerseAsset.red_letter_ranges when present; if absent, the verse renders with no red-letter styling (never guess).
Accessibility: interactive UI elements and navigational controls must provide meaningful semantics/contentDescription labels.
Theme defaults to system (SYSTEM). Manual override (LIGHT/DARK) must not alter stored data models beyond ReadingSettings.theme_override.
